title: Treegrid — placement of the hierarchical interactive data grid (a data-grid hierarchy projection)
summary: Prior-art survey grounding decision #1411 (surfaced by the ARIA-APG lens #1400): a treegrid is a 2-D grid whose rows form an expand/collapse hierarchy (file explorers, grouped data tables, nested org views) — the APG Treegrid pattern, with 2-D arrow navigation PLUS row expand/collapse. WE has data-grid (2-D grid nav) and tree-select (hierarchy) but no standard for their combination; the card flags this as the LOWEST-CONFIDENCE APG-harvest item. Native grounding: WAI-ARIA APG Treegrid (role=treegrid over role=row; parent rows carry aria-expanded omitted on leaves, aria-level 1-based, aria-posinset/aria-setsize); the keyboard model is the UNION of two WE already owns — Right expands a collapsed parent row OR moves a cell right, Left collapses OR moves left, Up/Down move across visible rows/cells and never change expand state; APG ships treegrid as its OWN pattern because it combines tree + grid, but that is an a11y-ROLE distinction, NOT evidence it must be a distinct BLOCK. Load-bearing finding: industry treats treegrid as a grid MODE/feature, not a separate component — AG Grid Tree Data + row grouping are modes of the grid (getDataPath/group columns, no separate component); MUI X DataGrid treeData + getTreeDataPath + row grouping are PROPS on the same grid; TanStack Table expansion is expanded state + getSubRows/getExpandedRowModel on the one table model; the counter-example PrimeReact TreeTable is a distinct component from DataTable but the split is driven by input DATA SHAPE (TreeNode[] vs flat rows), an impl/tooling convenience not a different a11y contract (impl-is-not-a-standard). Canonical vocabulary: treegrid (role), tree data / sub-rows / expandable rows (data shape), row grouping (aggregation variant), expander/toggler column, aria-level/expanded/posinset/setsize (all already WE vocabulary via the hierarchy intent). WE-tree: data-grid owns the 2-D half and is contractsOnly — its contractsOnly decision names 'the movement engine, the active-cell projection' as the seams against which competing implementations (virtualized rows, editable cells, server paging, 2-D selection) register WITHOUT changing the contract; composes Focus-Delegation orientation both; does NOT anticipate hierarchy/row-grouping anywhere (aria-rowcount/rowindex only) so the row-disclosure half is unowned by data-grid. hierarchy intent already owns the disclosure/hierarchy half AND explicitly names treegrid as a composer ('Tree-select, treegrid, and nested menus compose this one intent'): aria-level/setsize/posinset, the Right/Left expand-then-descend / collapse-then-ascend traversal, and the flatten-to-visible-rows projection ('Windowed Collection needs zero tree-specific changes'), collapse-focuses-ancestor; tree-select realizes it as a 1-D droplist member. Unowned residual: nobody owns the COMPOSITION itself — a 2-D grid whose rows are the hierarchy's flattened-visible projection and whose Right/Left arbitrates row-disclosure vs cell-movement (collapsed parent row + Right = expand; otherwise = move cell); that arbitration rule + the 'rows = flatten-to-visible projection feeding the grid's row axis' wiring is the only genuinely new contract — small, a projection over two existing seams, not a new engine. Recommendation: (Fork 1, ~70%) a hierarchy projection/dimension on data-grid (its row axis fed by the hierarchy intent's flatten-to-visible projection, rows carry aria-level/aria-expanded, the movement-engine seam gains the Right/Left disclosure-vs-cell arbitration rule; adds role=treegrid + composesIntents:[...,hierarchy]; no new block) — own-block is broken/over-minting (re-mints data-grid's 2-D engine + re-derives the hierarchy intent which already names treegrid as a composer; PrimeReact's separate TreeTable is a data-shape tooling convenience); doc-only composition under-delivers (the Right/Left arbitration rule is a real contract delta needing a named home + conformance vector) and is the fallback only if authoring shows zero contract change; honest residual — lowest-confidence APG-harvest item, the (a)/(b) line is thin and resolves at authoring time, own-block (c) firmly out (~90%). (Fork 2) row-grouping vs tree-data is likely NOT a fork (support-both): two data ADAPTERS onto the one hierarchy projection, not two contracts — one projection, two author-facing shapes. Supported by default: native role=treegrid + ARIA hierarchy attributes (already WE vocabulary); the APG Right/Left dual semantics (already in hierarchy.json; treegrid adds only the cell-movement fallback); the flatten projection (already owned); focus via data-grid's Focus-Delegation seam (orientation both); selection delegated to the Selection intent, independent of disclosure. Report: reports/2026-06-21-treegrid-hierarchical-data-grid.md.
